description: Domain pack for investigative journalists, newsrooms, and media researchers. Covers source verification, corporate ownership tracing, public records analysis, campaign finance mapping, leak analysis, and editorial security workflows.
author: CORTEX Intelligence Platform

entities.description: Elected or appointed government official with public duties and financial disclosures
Registered business entity — corporation, LLC, LP, or partnership
Entity with minimal operations suspected of existing primarily to obscure ownership or move funds
Political contribution from individual, PAC, or organization to candidate or committee
Freedom of Information Act request submitted to a government agency
Anonymized source node — identity compartmented, corroboration tracked separately
Government record obtained through FOIA, sunshine law, or public access portal
News article, broadcast segment, press release, or published analysis
Loan, bond, grant, government contract, or procurement award
Real estate parcel with ownership, assessment, and transaction history
Lawsuit, regulatory action, or criminal proceeding in any jurisdiction
PAC, super PAC, 527 organization, 501(c)(4), or party committee
Registered lobbyist or lobbying firm with client and issue disclosures
Individual who has reported wrongdoing through official channels or to media — identity protection paramount
Federal, state, or local procurement award with vendor and performance data
Tax-exempt entity with IRS Form 990 disclosure obligations
Filing with SEC, FCC, EPA, or other regulatory agency
Domain registration, social media account, email address, IP address, or other digital identifier
Set of documents obtained through unauthorized disclosure — metadata and authentication tracked
Person interviewed for the investigation — on or off record
Regulated facility with environmental permits, violations, and inspection history
Police department, sheriff's office, or federal law enforcement agency
Hospital, physician, pharmacy, or medical device company with payment and enforcement data
University, school district, or educational entity with governance and financial data
Dated event in the investigation narrative with source attribution
Natural person who ultimately owns or controls an entity, often hidden behind layers of corporate structure

categories.name: Corporate Malfeasance
categories.description: Fraud, embezzlement, securities violations, accounting manipulation, self-dealing

categories.name: Political Corruption
categories.description: Bribery, pay-to-play, revolving door, conflicts of interest, abuse of office

categories.name: Environmental Violations
categories.description: EPA enforcement, pollution, toxic releases, permit violations, contamination

categories.name: Human Rights
categories.description: Detention conditions, use of force, discrimination, labor exploitation, trafficking

categories.name: Campaign Finance
categories.description: Dark money, straw donors, coordination violations, super PAC abuse, foreign contributions

categories.name: Healthcare Fraud
categories.description: Medicare/Medicaid billing fraud, pharmaceutical kickbacks, clinical trial manipulation, opioid distribution

categories.name: National Security & Surveillance
categories.description: Surveillance programs, classification abuse, whistleblower retaliation, intelligence oversight failures

categories.name: Consumer Protection
categories.description: Product safety failures, deceptive practices, data breaches, privacy violations

categories.name: Housing & Real Estate
categories.description: Predatory lending, redlining, slumlord networks, code violations, gentrification displacement

categories.name: Education
categories.description: Title IX violations, endowment mismanagement, admissions fraud, research misconduct, campus safety

categories.name: Law Enforcement Accountability
categories.description: Use of force patterns, asset forfeiture, misconduct databases, qualified immunity, oversight failures

categories.name: Labor & Employment
categories.description: Wage theft, union busting, workplace safety violations, H-1B abuse, gig worker exploitation

categories.name: Nonprofit & Charity Fraud
categories.description: Self-dealing, inflated overhead, donor deception, governance failures, Form 990 analysis

categories.name: Technology & Surveillance
categories.description: Facial recognition, cell-site simulators, social media monitoring, AI bias, content moderation

categories.name: Infrastructure & Procurement
categories.description: No-bid contracts, cost overruns, revolving-door consultants, infrastructure failures, PPP abuse

categories.name: Media Freedom & Press Rights
categories.description: Reporter arrests, subpoenas for sources, SLAPP suits, prior restraint, press credential revocation

authority_patterns.name: Federal FOIA
authority_patterns.description: Freedom of Information Act — right to access federal agency records with 9 exemptions

authority_patterns.name: State Sunshine / Open Records Laws
authority_patterns.description: State-level public records access statutes — deadlines and exemptions vary by jurisdiction
examples: New York FOIL: Public Officers Law Art. 6

authority_patterns.name: SEC EDGAR Filings
authority_patterns.description: Mandatory corporate disclosures — 10-K annual reports, 10-Q quarterly, 8-K material events, proxy statements, insider trading forms

authority_patterns.name: FEC Campaign Finance Filings
authority_patterns.description: Campaign contribution and expenditure reports filed with Federal Election Commission

authority_patterns.name: Open Payments (Sunshine Act)
authority_patterns.description: Physician Payment Sunshine Act — mandatory disclosure of payments from drug/device manufacturers to physicians

authority_patterns.name: Corporate Transparency Act
authority_patterns.description: Beneficial ownership reporting to FinCEN — identifies natural persons behind LLCs and corporations

authority_patterns.name: PACER Federal Court Records
authority_patterns.description: Public Access to Court Electronic Records — federal court dockets, filings, and opinions

authority_patterns.name: USASpending Government Contracts
authority_patterns.citation: Federal Funding Accountability and Transparency Act
authority_patterns.description: Federal contract awards, grants, and financial assistance searchable by agency, vendor, and amount

authority_patterns.name: EPA ECHO Enforcement Data
authority_patterns.citation: Clean Air Act / Clean Water Act / RCRA
authority_patterns.description: EPA Enforcement and Compliance History Online — facility inspections, violations, and enforcement actions

authority_patterns.name: IRS Form 990 Nonprofit Disclosures
authority_patterns.description: Annual information returns for tax-exempt organizations — revenue, expenses, compensation, governance

authority_patterns.name: Lobbying Disclosure Act
authority_patterns.description: Quarterly lobbying activity reports — clients, issues, government contacts, compensation

authority_patterns.name: Reporter's Privilege / Shield Laws
authority_patterns.citation: Varies by state (no federal shield law)
authority_patterns.description: Statutory and common-law protections for journalists against compelled disclosure of confidential sources
authority_patterns.coverage: 49 states + DC have some form of shield law or reporter's privilege

focus_modes.name: Political Corruption
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing pay-to-play relationships, conflicts of interest, and abuse of public office

focus_modes.name: Corporate Ownership & Fraud
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing beneficial ownership chains, shell company structures, and corporate misconduct

focus_modes.name: Environmental Investigation
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing pollution, permit violations, enforcement actions, and contamination sites

focus_modes.name: Campaign Finance & Elections
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing money in politics — donor networks, PAC flows, dark money, and coordination

focus_modes.name: Housing & Real Estate
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing property ownership, slumlord networks, code violations, and lending patterns

focus_modes.name: Law Enforcement Accountability
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing use-of-force patterns, misconduct, settlements, and oversight failures

focus_modes.name: Healthcare Fraud & Pharma
focus_modes.description: Optimize for tracing pharmaceutical payments, billing fraud, and clinical trial manipulation

focus_modes.name: Leak Analysis & Whistleblower
focus_modes.description: Optimize for document authentication, metadata analysis, source protection, and publication chain tracking

investigation_checklist: Define the central question and scope
Map known relationships and financial flows
Submit FOIA requests to relevant agencies
Pull SEC EDGAR filings for corporate entities
Search FEC data for campaign finance connections
Search property records for real estate holdings
Red-team the story — what's the strongest counter-narrative?

source_reliability_scale.A: Completely reliable — established track record, documents corroborate
source_reliability_scale.B: Usually reliable — mostly corroborated, minor inconsistencies
source_reliability_scale.C: Fairly reliable — some corroboration, possible bias
source_reliability_scale.D: Not usually reliable — limited corroboration, known bias
source_reliability_scale.E: Unreliable — contradicted by evidence or known fabricator
source_reliability_scale.F: Cannot be judged — new source, insufficient basis for assessment
